query_description: Optimize a complex slow query using CTEs, window functions, and efficient joins
business_context: Refactor slow analytics query to run efficiently on large datasets while maintaining identical results
optimization_goals: Replace SELECT subqueries with window functions
Replace WHERE EXISTS with CTE + JOIN
validation_rules: Optimized query must return same columns as original
Number of rows must match original query
Data values must be identical (not just count)
Must use at least one CTE
Must use window functions (not SELECT subqueries)
Should not use correlated subqueries in WHERE
No syntax errors when running optimized query

optimization_areas: Removes SELECT subquery that runs per row
Uses CTEs to aggregate data once
Uses window functions for per-customer calculations

common_mistakes: Using SELECT subquery instead of window function
Not using PARTITION BY in window function
Using correlated subquery in WHERE still
Calculating aggregates in main SELECT instead of CTE
Not filtering based on CTE results

validation_approach.step_1: Run original slow query and count rows
validation_approach.step_2: Run optimized query and verify row count matches
validation_approach.step_3: Compare sample rows to verify data correctness
validation_approach.step_5: Note cost difference (optimized should be lower)
